steps.n: This is the basic form of the word that means a point in time

steps.m: when (introducing something certain to happen in the future)
steps.n: Adding ا at the end changes this from just meaning 'when' to specifically indicating something that will definitely happen in the future. In this ayah, it introduces what will happen when the sun is wrapped up.
The root letters represent the basic concept of sunlight and brightness
Root letters ش-م-س became the noun 'sun' by adding vowels. This sun is being darkened in the verse.
Added damma (ُ) at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence
Added 'al' to make it specific/definite - now it means 'the sun' not just any sun

steps.m: to roll up or wrap something into a round shape
steps.n: The root represents the basic idea of rolling or making something round
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to passive form with shadda to show something is being done intensively

steps.m: it (feminine) was intensively rolled up (referring to الشَّمْسُ/the sun)
steps.n: Added ت to show this is about the sun (which is feminine in Arabic). This word appears in the phrase 'When the sun is rolled up'
This is a word that shows when something happens. Here it introduces when the stars will scatter.

steps.m: and when (introducing the time of ٱنكَدَرَتْ)
steps.n: The و connects this condition with other events happening at the same time in neighboring verses
Connected the root letters with basic vowels to form the singular noun for one star
Makes it plural to show multiple stars by adding و and changing vowels to ُ
Added ٱل to make it specific (the stars), ُ to show it's the subject doing the action in the verse, and doubled the ن (shown by ّ) because it combines with the ل of ٱل
The root describes something becoming dark or scattered
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Added ان at the beginning to show the action happens by itself. This is used because the stars scatter on their own without something scattering them.
Changed initial alif to ٱ and added feminine past tense marker ت with sukun. The feminine form is used because النجوم (stars) is feminine in Arabic.
The base word إِذ means a point in time. Adding ا makes it specifically about future events.

steps.m: and when (connecting this future event to what came before)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this clause to what came before it. In this ayah, it connects to the previous signs of the Day of Judgment.
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to form the basic singular noun
This plural form is used for large groups of mountains
Adds ُ to show this word is the subject (doer) of the verb سُيِّرَتْ (were moved/set in motion)
Adds definite article to specify 'the mountains'
This root represents the basic concept of movement or travel
Connected the letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed the vowels and doubled the middle letter to show something was done to the mountains rather than them doing it themselves

steps.m: it (feminine) was made to move (referring to الْجِبَالُ/the mountains)
steps.n: Added ت to show this happened to the mountains (which is treated as feminine in Arabic)

steps.m: when (introduces the time when العِشَارُ will be abandoned)
steps.n: This is a word that shows when something will happen in the future. It connects to the next part of the ayah which talks about pregnant camels being abandoned.

steps.m: and when (introduces the time when العِشَارُ will be abandoned)
steps.n: The و connects this to the previous signs of the Day of Judgment mentioned before. It adds 'and' to show this is another sign.

steps.m: she-camels in tenth month of pregnancy
steps.n: Added the special pattern that makes this word mean pregnant she-camels. The ِ after ع and ا after ش create this specific meaning.

steps.m: the she-camels in the tenth month of pregnancy (subject of عُطِّلَتْ - were abandoned)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and ُ to show it's the subject doing the action in عُطِّلَتْ (were abandoned)
The basic meaning is about something being left unused
The shadda makes the middle letter stronger to show the action is done thoroughly
Changed the vowels to show this happened to something rather than something doing it
Added ت with sukun to show this happened to something feminine, like the pregnant camels mentioned before

steps.m: when (introduces the time when ٱلْوُحُوشُ حُشِرَتْ)
steps.n: This is a time word that shows when something happens. Here it introduces when the wild beasts will be gathered.

steps.m: and when (introduces the time when ٱلْوُحُوشُ حُشِرَتْ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this event with other events being described
The root refers to wildness or being untamed
Changed the internal vowels and added و to make it plural. This is a special plural form used for groups of wild animals.

steps.m: the wild beasts (subject of حُشِرَتْ - were gathered)
steps.n: ٱلْ makes it specific, ُ shows this is the subject being gathered in حُشِرَتْ (were gathered)
The word changes to show that the gathering happens to someone
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to show this happened to something rather than something doing it

steps.m: she/it was gathered (referring to الْوُحُوشُ - the wild beasts)
steps.n: Added ت with sukun to show this refers to a feminine subject (the wild beasts mentioned in the ayah)

steps.m: when (introduces the time when ٱلْبِحَارُ سُجِّرَتْ - the seas are heated)
steps.n: This is a word that shows when something happens in the future. Here it introduces when the seas will be heated.

steps.m: and when (introduces the time when ٱلْبِحَارُ سُجِّرَتْ - the seas are heated)
steps.n: The وَ at the beginning connects this event with other events mentioned before it, like making a list of things that will happen
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun
Changes the singular 'sea' into plural form for multiple seas

steps.m: the seas (subject of سُجِّرَتْ - were filled)
steps.n: ال makes it definite, ُ indicates nominative case as subject

steps.m: to fill, kindle, or heat up
steps.n: The basic meaning is to fill something to its maximum capacity
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to passive form and added intensity with shadda to show this was done with great force

steps.m: it (referring to البِحَارُ - the seas) was forcefully filled/heated
steps.n: Added feminine marker ت with sukun to show this happened to البِحَارُ (the seas) which is feminine plural in Arabic

steps.m: when (introduces the time when النُّفُوسُ souls will be paired)
steps.n: This is a word that shows when something will happen. Here it introduces when souls will be paired together.

steps.m: and when (introduces the time when النُّفُوسُ souls will be paired)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this with what came before it, like saying 'and' in English. This shows this event is part of a sequence of events being described.
The root refers to the essence of life - the breath or soul that makes something alive and precious
Changed the internal vowel pattern to show multiple souls. This word is the subject of زُوِّجَتْ (were paired) in this ayah.

steps.m: the souls (subject of زُوِّجَتْ - were paired)
steps.n: Adding the definite article ال makes the noun definite
Adding U sound at the end to show this word is doing the action
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the vowels to show this happened to someone rather than someone doing it

steps.m: she was joined/paired (referring to النُّفُوسُ - the souls)
steps.n: Added ت at the end to show this happened to something feminine (the souls)

steps.m: when (introducing the time when ٱلْمَوْءُۥدَةُ سُئِلَتْ)
steps.n: This is a word that shows when something happens. Here it introduces when the buried infant girl will be questioned.

steps.m: and when (introducing the time when ٱلْمَوْءُۥدَةُ سُئِلَتْ)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this to the previous statement, like saying 'and' in English
This root refers to the pre-Islamic practice of burying infant girls alive
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Changed from 'buried' to 'the buried one' by adding مَ at start and changing vowels to show who it happened to

steps.m: a female who was buried alive
steps.n: Added ة to show this specifically refers to a female victim

steps.m: the female who was buried alive
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to specify this particular victim, and ُ to show this is the subject of سُئِلَتْ (was asked about)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to show this happened to something rather than someone doing it

steps.m: she was asked (referring to الْمَوْءُۥدَةُ - the buried alive girl)
steps.n: Added تْ at the end to show this happened to a feminine subject (the buried alive girl mentioned in the ayah)
Letters أ-ي-ي combined with vowels to make أَيْ, a simple word for asking 'which'
The shadda emphasizes the question word, making it stronger

steps.m: which (in a possessive or connecting phrase)
steps.n: The kasra at the end shows this word is connected to what comes after it
Adding بِ at the beginning shows this is asking about the means or reason. In this ayah, it's asking about which sin was the cause.

steps.m: to commit a wrong or mistake

steps.n: Changed from action to thing by adding fatha and sukun. Like changing 'to wrong' into 'a wrong'.
Added tanwin (double kasra) to show this is any sin, not a specific one
The notes are incorrect - the only change is the position of the nunation mark (tanween) from above to below the letter ب. The meaning and grammatical function stay the same.
Changed vowels to show this happened TO someone rather than someone doing it

steps.m: she was killed violently (referring to المَوْءُۥدَةُ - the buried alive girl)
steps.n: Added ت with sukun at the end to show this happened to a feminine subject (the buried alive girl mentioned in the ayah)

Adding ا at the end makes this word specifically refer to future conditions, like saying 'when [something happens]'. This word introduces the condition of 'when the scrolls are spread out' in this verse.
The و connects this condition with what came before it in the text, showing these events happen in sequence
The root refers to writing or collecting written pages
Adds letters to make one page/scroll instead of many
Changes from a single page (صَحِيفَة) to many pages (صُحُف) by dropping the ة and changing the middle vowels

steps.m: the scrolls (subject of نُشِرَتْ - were spread out)
steps.n: Added ٱل to make it specific, shadda on ص because it's a sun letter, and ُ at the end because it's the subject of نُشِرَتْ (were spread out)
The basic meaning is to spread something out or unfold it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to show this happened to something rather than someone doing it

steps.m: it (f) was spread out (referring to الصُّحُفُ - the scrolls)
steps.n: Added تْ to show this happened to something feminine (the scrolls)

steps.m: when (introduces a condition about السَّمَآءُ كُشِطَتْ)
steps.n: This is a word that means 'when' and introduces a condition about something that will happen. In this ayah, it introduces what will happen when the sky is stripped away.

steps.m: and when (introduces a condition about السَّمَآءُ كُشِطَتْ)
steps.n: The و connects this condition with previous events being described in the surah. It shows this is part of a sequence of events that will happen.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changes from a verb meaning 'rise up' to become the noun 'sky' by adding اء at the end and dropping the و
Added 'the' (ٱل) at the start, which makes the س double (with shadda). The alif is written longer (آ) in this form.
Added ُ (damma) at the end to show this word is the subject of the verb كُشِطَتْ (was stripped away)

steps.m: to strip away or peel off
steps.n: The root describes the action of removing or peeling something away
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed the vowels to show this happened to something (passive voice)

steps.m: it (feminine) was stripped away (referring to السَّمَآءُ/the sky)
steps.n: Added ت to show this refers to the sky (which is feminine in Arabic)

steps.m: when (introducing the time when ٱلْجَحِيمُ سُعِّرَتْ)
steps.n: This is a word that shows when something happens. Here it introduces when the Hellfire will be set ablaze.

steps.m: and when (introducing the time when ٱلْجَحِيمُ سُعِّرَتْ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it, showing this is part of a sequence of events
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added ي and changed vowels to create a noun meaning something with intense quality

steps.m: the Hellfire (subject of سُعِّرَتْ - was kindled)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and ُ to show it's the subject of the verb سُعِّرَتْ (was kindled) in this ayah

steps.m: to set ablaze, to kindle/ignite fire
steps.n: The root relates to starting or kindling a fire
Basic past tense verb meaning 'he set ablaze' - root letters given vowels to make it a complete word
Changed to passive form - someone/something made it blaze (instead of it blazing on its own)
Added feminine marker to show it's talking about something feminine (like fire/hell)

steps.m: when (introduces what happens at a future time)
steps.n: This is a particle that introduces a time condition, specifically used for future events. In this ayah, it introduces what will happen on the Day of Judgment when Paradise is brought near.

steps.m: and when (connects to previous events on Day of Judgment)
steps.n: The و connects this event with other events described before it on the Day of Judgment, showing these things happen in sequence

steps.m: to cover or hide with vegetation
steps.n: The root relates to covering or concealing, which is why Paradise is called جنة - it's a garden with covering trees
The doubled ن makes the meaning stronger - like something being completely hidden
Added ة to make it a specific place with dense vegetation - a garden
Added ال to refer specifically to Paradise
Changed alif to hamzat wasl for smoother pronunciation when connecting words

steps.m: Paradise (subject of أُزْلِفَتْ - was brought near)
steps.n: Added damma to show this is the subject of the verb أُزْلِفَتْ (was brought near)
The basic meaning is about coming closer or approaching something

steps.m: was brought near (passive of bringing near)
steps.n: Changed to passive voice to show Paradise is being brought near by Allah, not bringing itself near. The أُ at the beginning and ِ in the middle show this is happening to Paradise rather than Paradise doing it.

steps.m: it (Paradise) was brought near (feminine past tense)
steps.n: Added feminine marker ت because الجنة (Paradise) is feminine in Arabic. This shows Paradise is what was brought near.

steps.m: it (Paradise) was brought near (feminine past tense)
steps.n: The sukun shows this is the basic form with no extra vowels at the end

steps.m: to know, have knowledge, be aware

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb. This word relates to نَفْسٌ (soul) in the ayah.
Added ت with sukun at the end to show the subject is feminine, matching with نَفْسٌ (soul) which is feminine in Arabic
Added vowel marks to make it a noun. This word is doing the action of knowing/learning (عَلِمَتْ) in this ayah.
Added double damma at the end to show this is the main doer of the action in the sentence.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that means 'what' or 'that which'. It connects two parts of a sentence, linking عَلِمَتْ (knew) with أَحْضَرَتْ (brought forth).
The shadda (doubled م) adds emphasis to the word, making its meaning stronger.
The alif is written with a special mark (آ) but this doesn't change the meaning - it's just a writing style.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Adding 'أ' at the start changes the meaning from 'he was present' to 'he made something/someone present' - like making something appear or bringing it
Adding تْ shows the subject is feminine singular (she). This verb is connected to عَلِمَتْ (she knew) in the ayah.
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation of the basic negation particle
The alif is written with a special mark (آ) to show it's pronounced longer for emphasis
Added فَ at the beginning to connect this negation to what came before. This word negates the verb أُقْسِمُ (I swear) that comes after it.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create past tense base form
Changed to present tense first person (I) form
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Changed vowels and added shadda to show multiple things that withdraw
Added ال to make it specific and ِ to show it follows something

steps.m: by the withdrawing ones (object of أُقْسِمُ)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show these withdrawing ones are what is being sworn by in the phrase فَلَآ أُقْسِمُ (so I swear)

steps.m: to move quickly (like running or flowing)

steps.n: Added ا after first letter to show who/what does the action. Added ِ at the end to show it's connected to another word.

steps.m: multiple things that move quickly (especially ships)
steps.n: Changed the middle letter to و and dropped the ي at the end to make it plural. This special plural form is often used for ships in Arabic.

steps.m: the ships (that are being talked about)
steps.n: Added ال at the beginning to show we're talking about specific ships, not just any ships
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to passive form by adding damma at start and doubling middle letter

steps.m: the planets that hide away (describing celestial bodies in ٱلْجَوَارِ)
steps.n: Added 'the' (ال) and changed the ending to match the word before it (planets that hide like shooting stars)
Connected the letters with fatha and sukun to make the basic noun
Added 'the' (ال) which combines with the ل to make one stronger ل sound
Added و as an oath particle (meaning 'by/I swear by') and kasra at the end because oath particles put words into the genitive case
This is a basic time word that points to when something happens. It's like saying 'at that time' or 'when' in English.

steps.m: when (referring to عَسْعَسَ - it becomes dark)
steps.n: Adding ا at the end makes it point to something that could happen in the future. In this ayah, it's talking about when the night becomes dark.

steps.m: it departed/came (referring to الَّيْلِ, the night)
steps.n: Added vowels to the root letters to make a word meaning when night comes or goes. This special word can mean both - when night arrives or leaves.
Changed the root letters into a noun for the time when light first appears
Added ال (the) and a kasra sound (ِ) at the end to show it's connected to 'when' (إِذَا)

steps.m: and the dawn (connected to إِذَا تَنَفَّسَ - when it breathes)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this with what came before. This word is part of an oath, swearing by the dawn when it breathes/spreads its light
This is the basic form of the word that points to a specific time
Adding ا at the end makes it refer to future or conditional time. Here it introduces the time when morning breathes (تَنَفَّسَ)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it into a basic past tense verb
The doubled middle letter makes the breathing action more intense
Adding ت shows the morning is doing the breathing action by itself
This is a special word that adds emphasis. It's like adding an exclamation mark in English to show something is definitely true.

Added هُ at the end to show we're talking about the Quran. This whole phrase connects to قَوْلُ (word/speech) in the rest of the ayah, telling us that it (the Quran) is truly the word of a noble messenger.
Added a small mark (ۥ) after هُ. This is just a writing helper that doesn't change the meaning.
Added لَ at beginning to add emphasis, especially since it comes after إِنَّ (indeed/verily)
Added ُ at the end because this word is the subject of إِنَّ (indeed) at the start of the verse
The و after س changes it from a verb (to send) into a noun (messenger)

steps.m: a messenger (who delivers the noble words)
steps.n: Added tanwin (two kasras) to show this is any messenger, not a specific one. This messenger is described as كَرِيمٍ (noble) in this ayah.

steps.m: to be noble, generous, or honorable

steps.n: This pattern creates an adjective showing a strong or permanent quality. In this ayah, it describes the messenger (رَسُولٍ) as being noble.
The tanween kasra (ٍ) shows this is describing something (the messenger) in a general way, like saying 'a noble messenger' instead of 'the noble messenger'
This is a special word that doesn't come from a root. It's used to show that something belongs to or is connected to something else.
Added kasra (ِ) to help with pronunciation
Added ى at the end to show this word connects to what comes after it. Here it connects to العَرْشِ (the throne) to mean 'owner of the throne'.
These are the basic letters that give us the idea of strength
Added damma on first letter and doubled the middle letter to make it into a noun meaning 'strength'
Added ة at the end to make it into a single instance of strength

steps.m: a strength (describing ذِى - one who has)
steps.n: Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this describes the word ذِى (one who has)
This word is used to show location or possession, like saying something is 'at' or 'with' someone
Added dots and marks to the basic letters to make a word meaning 'at' or 'with'

steps.m: in the presence of (ذِى ٱلْعَرْشِ - the Owner of the Throne)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to make it work as a preposition. This word connects to 'the Owner of the Throne' to show location.
This is the basic form of the word that shows possession or ownership
Added a small diagonal line (kasra) under the letter to connect it with the word 'power' that comes after it, showing who/what owns the power
Added ى at the end to complete the word. In this ayah, it describes someone who has power/strength (قُوَّةٍ)
Connected the root letters together and added vowel marks to make it a concrete noun - something you can see or touch
Added ٱلْ at the beginning to make it specific - we're talking about a particular throne, not just any throne
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to show this throne belongs to or is connected to ذِى (owner of) that comes before it in the ayah
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb

steps.m: firmly established (describing ذِى قُوَّةٍ - one of strength)
steps.n: Changed the pattern to show a strong quality. This describes the one mentioned in ذِى قُوَّةٍ (one of strength)

steps.m: firmly established (describing the noble messenger mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added tanwin (double kasra) to show this is describing the noble messenger mentioned earlier in the passage
Changed from a verb meaning 'to obey' into a noun meaning 'obedience' by adding vowels to the root letters
Added مُ at start to show someone is being obeyed. The و changes to ا to make it easier to say.
Added emphasis to show this is someone who is definitely obeyed

steps.m: one who is truly obeyed (in a general sense)
steps.n: Added tanween to show this describes someone in a general way
This is a basic word that points to a place. It's used to refer back to a location that was already mentioned or is understood from context.
Added vowels and ي to show this describes someone who is very trustworthy. This word describes جِبْرِيل (Gabriel) in this ayah.
Added tanwin (double kasra) to show this is describing Gabriel in a general way, emphasizing his trustworthy nature
This is a special word that can be used to ask 'what?' or to say 'not'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. In this ayah, it's negating 'your companion is not insane'
Changed to describe someone who does the action. This word is the subject of وَمَا (and not), showing what is being denied.
Added damma to show this is the main subject being talked about
Added كُم to show this companion belongs to those being addressed
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Shows someone affected by being hidden/covered (in mind), suggesting mental illness or possession in classical understanding
Adds -in sound at the end to make it 'any' hidden/covered one instead of 'the' hidden/covered one

steps.m: with/by any hidden/covered one (in mind) (describing صَاحِبُكُم)
steps.n: بِ adds meaning of 'with' or 'by'. This word describes صَاحِبُكُم (your companion) in the ayah, showing he is not possessed/mentally affected
This is a basic emphasis word that shows something definitely happened or will happen
Adding لَ makes the certainty even stronger, like saying 'most certainly' or 'without any doubt'

steps.m: and indeed certainly (connects to رَءَاهُ - he saw him)
steps.n: و connects this phrase to what comes after it (رَءَاهُ - he saw him), showing these events are linked together

steps.m: to see or perceive with eyes or mind

steps.n: Added fatha marks and changed ي to ى because it's at the end of the word. This makes it past tense.
Changed أ to ء and ى to ا because another suffix will be added

steps.m: he saw him (referring to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ seeing Angel Jibreel)
steps.n: Added هُ (hu) at the end to mean 'him' - changes 'he saw' to 'he saw him'
Added damma vowels to form the basic noun
Added ال with proper spelling as ٱلْ to make it definite

steps.m: in/at the horizon (location where رَءَاهُ/he saw him)
steps.n: Added بِ at beginning to show location, and ِ at end because بِ requires genitive case. This word shows where the seeing (رَءَاهُ) took place.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start to make it mean 'the one who' and changed inside vowels to show it's describing something that makes things clear

steps.m: the clear one (describing الأفق/the horizon mentioned before)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it specific and ِ at the end because it describes الأفق which has ِ
This is a negative particle that makes the following statement negative. In this ayah, it negates 'he is withholding about the unseen'

steps.m: and not (negates هُوَ عَلَى ٱلْغَيْبِ بِضَنِينٍ)
steps.n: The و connects this negation to the previous statement, showing these ideas are linked together
This is a basic pronoun meaning 'he' - one of the most common words in Arabic. In this ayah, it refers to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ.
Added the vowel marks ُ (damma) on ه and َ (fatha) on و to show the correct pronunciation
Form used for feminine superlative (meaning 'highest')

steps.m: upon (connecting غَيْبِ with what comes before it)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation. In this ayah, عَلَى connects the word غَيْبِ (unseen) with what comes before it, showing the relationship between them.

steps.m: to be absent or hidden from sight
steps.n: This form turns the concept of being hidden into a noun meaning 'the unseen'
Turns the root letters into a simple noun meaning 'something hidden'

steps.m: the unseen (possessed by or connected to something after it)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and ِ at the end to show it's connected to what comes after it in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed the word to describe someone who does this action as a characteristic. This is describing Prophet Muhammad ﷺ in this ayah, saying that he is not someone who withholds knowledge.

steps.m: with/by one who withholds (describing Muhammad ﷺ in عَلَى ٱلْغَيْبِ بِضَنِينٍ)
steps.n: Added بِ at the start to show 'with/by' and tanwin at the end. In this ayah, it's saying that Muhammad ﷺ is not someone who withholds knowledge of the unseen (الغيب).
This is a negative particle that makes the following statement negative: 'it is not the word of an accursed devil'

steps.m: and not (negates هُوَ بِقَوْلِ شَيْطٰنٍ رَّجِيمٍ)
steps.n: The و connects this negation to what came before it, adding 'and' to show continuation of thought

steps.m: he/it (referring to the Quran in this ayah)
steps.n: This is a basic pronoun used to refer to a masculine singular person or thing. In this ayah, it refers back to the Quran, connecting to the previous verses.

steps.m: speech/saying (object of بِ and owned by شَيْطٰنٍ)
steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'speech'. This word is connected to شَيْطٰنٍ (satan) to mean 'speech of satan', and is affected by the preposition بِ.

steps.m: speech/saying (object of بِ and owned by شَيْطٰنٍ)
steps.n: The ِ (kasra) at the end shows this word is connected to the word before it (بِ) and belongs to شَيْطٰنٍ (Satan)
Added بِ at the beginning to mean 'by/with'. This connects to قَوْلِ to show how something is done.
Root letters got extra letters to make it mean 'one who is rebellious' instead of just 'being rebellious'
The tanwin (double kasra) shows this is indefinite (any devil) and shows it follows the word قَوْلِ (speech of)

steps.m: to drive away by throwing stones, to curse
one who is cursed and driven away
steps.n: Added vowels and ي to show this describes someone who receives the action of being cursed and driven away

steps.m: of one who is cursed and driven away
steps.n: Added the genitive case ending to show this word is being described or possessed

steps.m: intensely cursed and driven away (describing شَيْطٰنٍ/Satan)
steps.n: The letter ر has a shaddah (doubled), making the word stronger to emphasize how cursed Satan is
This is a question word that asks about location (specifically asking about where تَذْهَبُونَ (you are going))
The فَ prefix shows this question follows from what was mentioned before, expressing surprise or disbelief. It connects to تَذْهَبُونَ (you are going) to ask 'so where are you going?'

steps.m: to go/move from one place to another
steps.n: The basic meaning of going or departing from one place to another
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' as subject, changed vowels to show present tense

steps.m: you all go (after فَأَيْنَ - so where)
steps.n: Added ونَ at the end to show that multiple people are being addressed. This verb comes after فَأَيْنَ (so where) to ask 'so where are you all going?'

steps.m: not (negating هُوَ in هُوَ إِلَّا ذِكْرٌ)
steps.n: This is a negation particle that means 'not'. In this ayah, it negates هُوَ (it/he) in the phrase هُوَ إِلَّا ذِكْرٌ (it is nothing but a reminder). The إِلَّا (except/but) that follows it creates the meaning 'nothing but' or 'only'.

steps.m: he/it (referring to the Quran in this ayah)
steps.n: This is a pronoun that points to something being talked about. In this ayah, it refers back to the Quran.
This is the basic form of the word that shows something is being excluded

steps.m: except (excluding ذِكْرٌ 'a reminder' from what came before)
steps.n: Adding the shadda and alif makes the exclusion stronger and complete. In this ayah, it's excluding 'a reminder' from whatever was mentioned before.

steps.m: remembrance (that is being described in هُوَ)
steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'remembrance'. This word is being described by هُوَ (it) in this ayah.

steps.m: a remembrance (that is being described in هُوَ)
steps.n: Added tanween (double damma) to show this is indefinite, meaning 'a remembrance' rather than 'the remembrance'

steps.m: to know deeply or mark something to recognize it

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to know' into noun 'world' - what is known/marked
Added ين at the end to make it plural (more than two worlds/realms)
Added لِلْ (for the) at the beginning. The ل means 'for' and the لْ is a shortened form of ال (the)

steps.m: for the worlds/realms (related to ذِكْرٌ - a reminder)
steps.n: Added shadda on ل and elongated alif (ٰ). This word is connected to ذِكْرٌ (reminder) in the ayah, showing that the reminder is for all worlds/realms.
This is a combination of two particles: لِ (for/to) and مَن (who/whoever)
Added short vowels (fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he wanted'
Changed to a hollow verb pattern by adding long alif
Basic preposition meaning 'from' - shows where something starts or comes from

steps.m: from you all (who want أَن يَسْتَقِيمَ)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being addressed. This refers to those who want to take the straight path (يَسْتَقِيمَ) mentioned right after.

steps.m: that (connects to يَسْتَقِيمَ - to be upright)
steps.n: This is a particle that connects to the verb after it (يَسْتَقِيمَ) to show its purpose or goal. It's like saying 'in order to' or 'so that' in English.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added است at the beginning to show seeking or trying to achieve straightness
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
This is a negative particle that negates the verb that follows it (تَشَآءُونَ). In this context it means 'not' rather than 'what'
The و (and) is added at the beginning to connect this negation with what came before it. Together with the verb after it, it means 'and you will not'
This is the basic meaning of wanting or wishing something
Connected the root letters and added vowels. The ي becomes ا because it's a weak letter.
The ُ at the end shows it's happening now (present tense)
Adding ون makes it refer to a group of people instead of one person
The words are the same - just written slightly differently. Both mean 'you all want' and already have ون showing it's for a group
This is the basic form of the word that means 'except'. In this ayah, it connects what comes before (your will/wanting) with what comes after (Allah's will).
The alif is written with a special mark (آ) to show it's pronounced longer. This doesn't change the meaning, just the pronunciation.
This is a connecting particle that joins two parts of a sentence. Here it connects the previous part about 'willing/wanting' to what Allah wills. It shows that one action depends on another.
This is the basic meaning of wanting or wishing something
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make past tense verb
The weak letter ي changes to ا, which is a common change in Arabic verbs
Adding يَ at the start changes it from past to present tense. This verb comes after إِلَّآ أَن (except that) in the ayah.
Adding the long vowel ا changes the word pattern to match present tense
Added ال to specify this is THE one true deity, and the hamza was dropped because this is a special divine name

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of يَشَآءَ - He wills)
steps.n: Allah's name gets a stronger L sound (shadda), starts with a connecting alif, and ends with U sound to show Allah is doing the action
This root is about caring for and nurturing something
The doubling of ب with shadda and the fatha on ر changes this from an action into someone who does that action completely
The damma at the end shows this word is the subject (doer) of the verb يَشَآءَ (wills/wants) in this ayah

steps.m: to know deeply/to be certain of
steps.n: This root is about knowledge and learning
Root 'know' becomes 'world' by adding alif and fatha - like how a world is something we can know and see around us
Added ين to make it plural. The ِ before ين shows it's owned by رَبُّ (Lord of...)
Added 'ال' (the) at the start. The long alif (ٰ) is just a different way to write 'عَا'. This word is connected to 'رَبُّ' (Lord) to mean 'of all worlds'
